mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.5% do not significantly exceed baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above 9.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE > 9.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that compresses equity valuations and forward earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 555k units suggests moderate demand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances (both RSAFS and HOUST declining simultaneously), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production below 94.5 signals supply-side weakness that constrains corporate output and profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean return > 0 with t-test p > 0.10) when INDPRO < 94.5, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint expansion in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals robust labor market tightening that increases wage pressures and inflation expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to higher discount rates.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 200k month-over-month AND UNRATE declines > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130.7M and UNRATE at 9.4% show mixed signals
regime_hint: tight labor market with inflation risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) expanding above trend signals strong aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 0 with t-test p > 0.10) during periods of GDPC1 growth, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 1.0%) signals monetary tightening that increases real discount rates and reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between inflation and rates is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 500k units signals weakening residential investment and forward demand weakness that constrains GDP growth and equity earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 555k units is above threshold but vulnerable
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean return > 0 with t-test p > 0.10) when HOUST < 500k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
